title: Onobroma arborescens
intro: Onobroma arborescens, also known as Inga tree or Onobroma, is a species of flowering plant in the family Malpighiaceae. The health benefits of Onobroma arborescens are attributed to its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Onobroma arborescens is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used as a component in various herbal remedies, such as cough syrups and expectorants.
